replies.text: ​@strikeforcealpha9343 don't feel sorry for people either not using their brains or not having any in the first place. If I got sent a script and it said Star Trek for a title and had this level of shyt everything about it. Even for my first acting job ever or for some of these older well established Actors, no way in Hell would I commit career suicide by accepting any role in any project like this. And if the Show Runners pull a bait and switch in production they violated my contract and I walk with my money. Or I give them the initial pay I received back to them and I walk either way. No one is holding a gun to their heads forcing them to take bad roles in even worse projects.

replies.author: @headp3
replies.text:
Well we got most of the OG series, and Tea Time with Picard writes itself. Jordy in pitch black, See the World through my Eyes😂, Worf in a WWE Wrestling Ring, your Self Defense Training. Dr Crusher holding Wesley with tape over his mouth, because the Shut Up Wesley stream. Diana put her in the Kitchen making Daddy a Sandwich, or a bag over her head since the new show thinks Telepaths, Empathy and Deff people are all the same and interchangeable. Riker in the Riker chair stance. Data surrounded by Chess Boards, cause we playing 4D Chess with these Lefty freaks at this point. Just light Ginen on fire and throw it in the trash, cause Whoopie is a scum human and that's where garbage belongs. Obviously Barkley on a Snowflake cause he is the perfect example of the Left in many ways, minus intelligence.

DS9 Odo, but half way through replace the figurine with Silly Putty, Sisco with a Baseball and a Cross, just to piss of the left and imply "The Prophets" we're in fact Christians😂 Dax next to a tube of Lube and ashes cause she hot and old as dust. O'Brian and Bashier in a Scissors Pose, since somehow O'Brain switched from loving and caring about his Wife and Daughter, to "Struggle Sessions" with Bashier. Quark on a pile of money because Capitalism Rules! Kira, aka the Karen of DS9, obviously her holding a sign asking to speak to the Manager.

Voyager, Janeway holding 2 signs. One says, "I'm the Captain now" the other "I am the Karen!" Chikotea sitting on a Slot Machine with a "Peace Pipe" and an empty bottle of Jack Daniels. Paris in front of the Flash Gordon DVD, since they thought anything to do with him was somehow retro for us, much less hundreds of years in the future. Kim can stand in the corner and feel sorry for himself for no reason. Tuvoc, have Spock kick his ass for being a shyt Vulcan, and a worse human being in real life. Balana and a Spicy Taco, cause somehow Klingons became feisty Mexicans in how they act. 7 of 9 Jello Wrestling! Nelix shoved head first into the ground cause his character was as exciting as a Potato. Kess attach her to a Firework and watch her "Ascend" to a Being of pure light like the show did, cause heaven forbid a TV show has more than 2 attractive Wemon in it at one time. That's about all I can stand to go through for shows, from Voyager on you might as well just take everything past that point throw it all into a 50 Gallon Steel drum with a Gallon of Gasoline and light that crap in fire!

author: @TVSanFierro
text: I honestly feel like whoever wrote Star Trek: Starfleet Academy has either never watched Star Trek, or watched it without paying attention. In the TNG episode Coming of Age, they already showed us what Starfleet Academy cadets are supposed to be like, and it is very clear that the entrance exams are brutal, and that only the absolute best minds even get a chance. Starfleet Academy was disciplined and demanding and now we have cadets eating they're combadge while every second instructor is overweight. No discipline, no standards, just using sci-fi shortcuts to “fix” the problem by making a few of them have Thor's mighty powers so the weight is not a problem. The values, the tone, and the competence that defined Starfleet are completely gone and it is funny how they are disciplining cadets while the Academy Chancellor herself has none of it. Walking around barefoot, curling up in the captain's chair like a cat, the whole thing feels less like Star Trek and more like Idiocracy set in the Star Trek universe.

author: @Harrison11106
text: I'm probably dating myself with the following, but here goes:  the kicker is that there WAS a Starfleet Academy proposal that could've worked.  I remember a year or 2 after Rodenberry died, that one of the proposals was that we, as the audience, would follow a group of cadets for 2 to 4 seasons.  The impression I got was that it'd be an anthology kind of thing. We'd follow them from their inauguration & on as they dealt with their trials & tribulations.  When they graduated, or washed out (yes, we'd see that too), they'd be assigned to their respective ships, spaceports, & whatever in-between.  Then we'd get a new group of would-be cadets to follow, & the process would start over again.  I liked that idea.  Such a pity that THIS was what we got instead.

replies.author: @davidford3115
replies.text: The anime "Starship Operators" executed this concept so much better.  And for bonus points, their uniforms are also color-coded by department.  Command Bridge staff is Blue, Tactical Bridge is Orange, Navigation Bridge is Red, and the single medical along with the logistics staff wear purple.
